RACE REPLAY: Jayco Herald Sun Tour 2020 Stage 2 | Beechworth - Falls Creek

transcript.clean_text: we're into the hills for stage two of the Santos Tour Down Under after four opening laps around the township of Woodside which makes its first appearance at the race route had southwestern into familiar Tour Down Under territory as the peloton traverses a lumpy finishing circuit around sterling a further four times race director Mike Turtur says it's one for the all rounders so time will tell if Sam Bennett will still be holding the okie leader's jersey tomorrow we had attacks right from kilometer zero as yesterday's most combative rider Joey Ross was at it again followed by uni sa Sam Jenna he took the first kom at kilometer 15 and soon after Laurens Therese of Astana an Omer Goldstein of Israel start-up nation had successfully bridged across from the peloton a couple more riders in the break was not going to deter the ccc rider from sweeping up maximum bonuses once again of the second king of the mountain checkpoint the climb of Quarry Road yielded 10 points apiece at the top so Ross hopped now earns himself a handy lead in that competition this break was on a short leash their lead was never going above three minutes all day and with two laps of the finishing circuit remaining it was down to less than 30 seconds this was the point that DeVries decided to go solo but it was never going to be an easy task to hold off a fresh and motivated peloton led by the team of the overall leader to Kearney quickstep much room to maneuver up this gradual climb to the finish and we had a few mishaps on the way Marc sorrow and Fabio Sabatini getting tangled up here and holding up the spending champion Daryl Impey everybody able to dust themselves down and get on their way after that one though and we were ready for the last of these finishing circuits a poorly timed puncture furrow and Dennis saw him need to get pasted back to the bunch fortunately he had a reliable in EOS wheel to follow in a loop row it was shoulders to shoulders stuff at the head of the race with the favourites jostling for every inch of road certainly doesn't take long for those racing instincts to return after a long winter not a great day for Roman bard a mechanical problems with dentist chances of a good result today another crash with 1.4 kilometers to go and this was a big one almost half the field caught up in one way or another but with the three kilometer rule in play they would at least not be penalized with time losses at the finish under a rifle however right in the middle and we'd have no chance of contesting the finish today ahead and mitchelton Scott were busy setting up Daryl Impey with Lucas Hamilton peeling off having done his turn through comes new Australian National Champion cam Meyer sweeping on the other side of the road though a Michael Moore coup with the ochre Jersey Sam Bennett in tow a tantalizing finish in store MP deciding to go long on the right hand boards trapped by Nathan hazard covetous and as the camera zoomed out we were fully expecting to see Bennett in full flow but no a shake of the head and the Irishman was done out of his slipstream seemingly from nowhere comes the diminutive Caleb Ewing in the black and red making light work of the uphill dragged the line and storming to the wind the aerial shot demonstrates an electrifying turn of pace from the Aussie always putting time into his fellow sprinters by the finish he won the stage inter sterling back in 2018 and after yesterday's disappointment he'll be delighted with this response here to top 10 and Ewan takes the stage with bonus seconds and count back enabling him to leapfrog Sam Bennett into the overall lead to precious bonus seconds at the finish as well for MP and Haas who will be eyeing this oka Jersey themselves in the days to come this man will enjoy his moment but it could be all change again tomorrow the Tour Down Under visits para kun for stage 3 starting from unli its back into the hills via radar cuddly Creek and four laps around para kun before looping background for the finished climb of torrents Hill just over a kilometer long averaging 9.3% with maximum grades of 20 those numbers don't get you excited for tomorrow I don't know what we'll see you then
